title: api-contracts schema additions — rc.2/rc.3 MCP protocol pre-lock
description: Pre-register MCP tool input/output Zod schemas for rc.2 (`fab_extract_knowledge`) and rc.3 (`fab_review`), plus extend existing `fab_plan_context` / `fab_get_rule_sections` schemas with grill-locked Q6 fields (tags index, layer_filter, redirect_to error). This is the protocol-lock task: schemas land NOW so consumers can target them; implementations land in rc.2/rc.3. (1) Add `tags?: string[]` to `RuleDescriptionIndexItem` (rc.2-prep already added the frontmatter `tags` field; this exposes it in the index API). (2) Add `layer_filter?: z.enum(['team','personal','both'])` to `PlanContextInputSchema`. (3) Add `redirect_to?: z.object({ stable_id: z.string() })` to `GetRuleSectionsResultSchema` error union — fired post-layer-flip when stable_id changed. (4) Add `FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema` (semi-thick: `{ source_session, recent_paths[], user_messages_summary, type, slug }`) + `FabExtractKnowledgeOutputSchema` (`{ pending_path, idempotency_key }`). (5) Add `FabReviewInputSchema` as `z.discriminatedUnion('action', [...6 actions: list / approve / reject / modify / search / defer])` + `FabReviewOutputSchema`.

files.target: RuleDescriptionIndexItem + PlanContextInputSchema + GetRuleSectionsResultSchema + new FabExtractKnowledge + new FabReview schemas
files.change: (1) Add `tags: z.array(z.string()).optional()` to RuleDescriptionIndexItem; (2) Add `layer_filter: z.enum(['team','personal','both']).optional()` to PlanContextInputSchema; (3) Add `redirect_to: z.object({ stable_id: z.string() }).optional()` to GetRuleSectionsResultSchema error shape; (4) Add FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema z.object({ source_session, recent_paths, user_messages_summary, type, slug }) + FabExtractKnowledgeOutputSchema z.object({ pending_path, idempotency_key }); (5) Add FabReviewInputSchema as discriminated union over action enum (list/approve/reject/modify/search/defer) — approve uses pending_paths[], modify allows layer field for layer-flip, defer accepts reason+until — plus FabReviewOutputSchema with corresponding result shapes

files.target: new test cases for added/extended schemas
files.change: Add (a) RuleDescriptionIndexItem with/without tags roundtrip; (b) PlanContextInput with layer_filter='team'/'personal'/'both' and missing (default behavior assertion); (c) GetRuleSectionsResult with redirect_to populated; (d) FabExtractKnowledgeInput valid + invalid (missing required field) cases; (e) FabReviewInput discriminated-union — 1 valid case per action (6 cases) + 1 invalid (unknown action); (f) FabReviewOutput shape matches each action's result

implementation: Step 1 — Index extension: open RuleDescriptionIndexItem definition, add `tags: z.array(z.string()).optional()` field; ensure consumers reading the index handle missing tags as []

Step 2 — PlanContextInput extension: add `layer_filter: z.enum(['team','personal','both']).optional()` field with inline comment explaining default behavior comes from fabric-config.default_layer_filter

Step 3 — GetRuleSectionsResult extension: locate error/discriminated branch (likely GetRuleSectionsResultSchema or its NotFoundResult variant), add `redirect_to: z.object({ stable_id: z.string() }).optional()` field; document semantic: present iff post-layer-flip stable_id changed

Step 4 — New FabExtractKnowledge schemas: define FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema = z.object({ source_session: z.string(), recent_paths: z.array(z.string()), user_messages_summary: z.string(), type: z.enum([5 types]), slug: z.string() }); FabExtractKnowledgeOutputSchema = z.object({ pending_path: z.string(), idempotency_key: z.string() }); export both via index.ts barrel

Step 5 — New FabReview schemas: define FabReviewInputSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('action', [ z.object({action:z.literal('list'), filters:...}), z.object({action:z.literal('approve'), pending_paths:z.array(z.string())}), z.object({action:z.literal('reject'), pending_paths:..., reason:z.string()}), z.object({action:z.literal('modify'), pending_path:..., changes:z.object({title?,summary?,layer?,maturity?,tags?})}), z.object({action:z.literal('search'), query:z.string(), filters?}), z.object({action:z.literal('defer'), pending_paths:..., until:z.string().datetime().optional(), reason?}) ]); FabReviewOutputSchema corresponds with 1 result shape per action; export both

Step 6 — Tests: write ≥ 1 valid + 1 invalid case per new schema; for FabReviewInput, write 6 valid (one per action) + 1 invalid (unknown action) cases; for FabExtractKnowledgeInput, write 1 valid + 1 invalid (missing source_session)

Step 7 — Backward-compat regression: load existing api-contracts fixtures (if present) or hand-craft minimal valid PlanContextInput / GetRuleSectionsResult and confirm they still parse with new optional fields absent
Step 8 — Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared build && pnpm --filter @fabric/shared test` — confirm all green; eyeball coverage ≥ 90% on added schemas

reference.pattern: Pre-lock MCP API protocol via Zod schemas before implementation lands; discriminated union for multi-action input
reference.examples: Follow rc.2-prep schema-additions pattern (commit a85121a) for atomic schema-only commits with full test coverage

criteria: Each new schema has ≥ 1 valid + 1 invalid test case; FabReviewInput has 6 valid (one per action) + 1 invalid

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: implementing the MCP tools themselves (rc.2 fab_extract_knowledge, rc.3 fab_review) — this task lands ONLY the Zod schemas; modifying fabric-config (TASK-002); modifying event-ledger (TASK-004); docs (TASK-006). Strictly: schema definition + test coverage.

rationale.chosen_approach: Pre-lock the rc.2/rc.3 MCP protocol via Zod schemas before implementation, so future TASK can develop against typed contracts; use discriminated union for fab_review's 6 actions to enable exhaustive narrowing
alternatives_considered: Defer schema definition to rc.2/rc.3 (loses pre-lock benefit, risks last-minute API churn)
Separate schemas per action (verbose, hard to maintain consumer code)
decision_factors: Discriminated union enables consumer-side type narrowing on `action` field
Single shared package = single source of truth for MCP server + Skill consumers
rationale.tradeoffs: Some schemas land before consumers exist (TS check ensures they're well-formed but no runtime validation until rc.2/3) — accepted because schemas are easier to refine pre-implementation than post-

manual_checks: Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared build` — confirm no TS inference errors on the new discriminated union
Manually call FabReviewInputSchema.parse({action:'modify', pending_path:'x', changes:{layer:'personal'}}) — confirm OK
Manually parse legacy PlanContextInput JSON without layer_filter — confirm parses cleanly
success_metrics: All new test cases pass; existing tests still pass
Discriminated union narrowing works in TS consumer code (manually verify by writing a small switch on action)
unit: PlanContextInput layer_filter all 3 enum values + missing
FabReviewInput 6 valid (one per action) + 1 invalid (unknown action)
FabReviewOutput shape matches each action's result

risks.description: Discriminated union for fab_review introduces complex generic narrowing that confuses downstream TS consumers
risks.mitigation: Provide a small narrowing helper or example in JSDoc on FabReviewInputSchema; ensure tests exercise narrowing via a switch
risks.fallback: If narrowing pain manifests in rc.3 implementation, refactor to per-action explicit schemas with a shared base

risks.description: Schema lands before MCP implementation, risking divergence if rc.2/3 implementation needs different shape
risks.mitigation: Document in docs/mcp-contracts.md (TASK-006) that schemas may evolve in rc.2/3 if implementation reveals gaps; treat as known risk
risks.fallback: Schema breaking changes in rc.2/3 are acceptable pre-2.0 stable (per clean-slate memory)

interfaces.name: FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema
interfaces.definition: z.object({ source_session: z.string(), recent_paths: z.array(z.string()), user_messages_summary: z.string(), type: z.enum(['decisions','pitfalls','guidelines','models','processes']), slug: z.string() })
interfaces.purpose: rc.2 MCP tool input — semi-thick (Skill summarizes, MCP persists)

interfaces.name: FabReviewInputSchema
interfaces.definition: z.discriminatedUnion('action', [list, approve, reject, modify, search, defer])
interfaces.purpose: rc.3 MCP tool input — 6 actions exhaustively narrowed by discriminator
